topic: Mine OpenLTM (open-source long-term-memory plugin for AI coding agents; Bun/TypeScript/SQLite/FTS5; MIT) for design teachings transferable to the LOCAL single-user system-spec-kit Memory system. Find what OpenLTM does DIFFERENTLY than us and what we could copy/learn from to improve system-spec-kit memory (or related systems). Per-teaching adopt/adapt/reject/defer verdict with risk, mapped to a system-spec-kit surface or a proposed new sub-packet. Negative knowledge (what NOT to copy given arch/scale/design mismatch) is first-class.

orchestration.rationale: Executors run READ-ONLY analysis only; orchestrator writes all iteration/delta/state artifacts. Sidesteps the project Gate-3 executor-write HARD BLOCK, is compaction-safe, and honors the operator's standing parallel-fanout preference. 10 iterations = 10 independent OpenLTM subsystems analyzed in concurrent batches, then a synthesis + adversarial-verify pass.

executor.dispatchPattern: read-only-analysis; orchestrator-writes-state (Gate-3-safe, race-safe); narrow per-subsystem scope to fit the xhigh timeout

researchTarget.name: OpenLTM
researchTarget.kind: open-source long-term-memory plugin for AI coding agents (Claude Code / OpenCode / Pi); Bun + TypeScript; SQLite (WAL) + FTS5(BM25) + optional sqlite-vec; pluggable embedding providers; 4 lifecycle hooks; janitor maintenance; typed knowledge graph; Next.js graph visualizer; ~29K LOC; v2.x
researchTarget.license: MIT (design inspiration only — no code copying)

consumer.name: system-spec-kit Memory
consumer.kind: LOCAL single-user / single-tenant continuity store (SQLite index + separate vector store; ollama nomic 768d); semantic+lexical trigger matching; importance tiers; causal edges; FSRS decay/co-activation; self-maintaining incremental index; shadow-first learning-feedback reducers; spec-folder continuity ladder; 37-tool MCP surface

continuation.rationale: Operator flagged that our memory is spec-doc-based while OpenLTM is row-based; several round-1 teachings assumed a row-write model. Re-test transferability under the corrected architecture.
